purpose: No-GPU structural dry-check that the gpuwrf-canary-d02-sample asset is ready for the binding v0.9.0 naive-agent README gate. Confirms: (1) the sample namelist validates fail-closed (all schemes supported); (2) the gpuwrf CLI accepts the sample paths and passes all cheap validation + the registry check up to the heavy GPU pipeline boundary; (3) the packaged input is internally dim-consistent and consistent with the CPU-WRF reference. NO GPU and NO timestep was run (the GPU is held by another lane); the actual GPU forecast-vs-CPU comparison is the tag-time manager step.

checks.name: Sample namelist passes validate_supported_namelist (all schemes supported)
checks.detail: validate_supported_namelist(<sample>/namelist.input) returned without raising. mp=8, bl=5, sfclay=5, land=4, ra_lw=4, ra_sw=4, cu=1/0, sf_urban=0, diff_6th_opt=2, w_damping=1, damp_opt=3 all in the trunk-0.9.0 accepted registry; diff_opt=0/km_opt=0 (swapped from CPU-WRF 1/4) are in {0,2}/{0,1}.

checks.name: gpuwrf run accepts the sample paths and passes cheap validation + registry up to the heavy GPU import
checks.detail: cli.main(['run','--namelist',<sample>/namelist.input,'--input-dir',<sample>,'--output-dir',...,'--domain','d02','--hours','1','--compare-cpu-dir',<sample>]) passed input-dir-exists, namelist==<input-dir>/namelist.input, hours>0, compare-dir-exists, and the fail-closed namelist registry check, then reached DailyPipelineConfig(...) construction (cli.py line 275). The heavy gpuwrf.integration.daily_pipeline import was stubbed to raise so NO JAX/GPU ran; reaching that line proves all pre-GPU validation accepted the sample.

checks.name: gpuwrf run --help builds and exposes the gate args
Namelist d02 grid consistent with packaged wrfout d02 dims
checks.detail: namelist d02 e_we=160/e_sn=67/e_vert=45 maps to wrfout west_east_stag=160, south_north_stag=67, bottom_top_stag=45 and west_east=159/south_north=66/bottom_top=44 (staggered = e_*, unstaggered = e_*-1). Consistent.

checks.name: All packaged d02 wrfout frames share identical dims; CLI compare helper returns PASS
checks.detail: The three packaged wrfout_d02 frames (t=0,1h,2h) have byte-identical dimension dictionaries. cli.compare_wrfout_dimensions on the t=1h file against the sample dir returned status=PASS (self-reference).

checks.name: Raw CPU-WRF namelist (diff_opt=1/km_opt=4) is REJECTED by the CLI
checks.detail: Running the CLI with namelist.input.cpu-wrf-original content returned exit code 2 with the two expected UnsupportedNamelistOption failures (diff_opt=1, km_opt=4). Confirms the gate is genuinely fail-closed and the diff_opt/km_opt swap was necessary, not cosmetic.

checks.name: --namelist not equal to <input-dir>/namelist.input is REJECTED
checks.detail: Pointing --namelist at namelist.input.cpu-wrf-original (not <input-dir>/namelist.input) returned exit code 2 with the path-mismatch error. Confirms the CLI's P0 namelist-path rule.

checks.name: Extracted tarball is self-contained: namelist validates and CLI accepts it
checks.detail: Extracting gpuwrf-canary-d02-sample.tgz to a fresh temp dir and re-running C1 (namelist validates) and C2 (CLI reaches heavy import) both passed against the extracted copy. The asset is self-contained as a clean-room agent would experience it.

explicitly_not_done: No GPU forecast was run (no timestep advanced); the GPU is held by another lane.
No value/RMSE comparison; only the structural dimension-compare path was exercised (self-reference) and the namelist + CLI acceptance.
The asset was NOT uploaded to GitHub; download_url is a tag-time placeholder.
binding_gate_remaining: On a GPU: extract asset, pip install -e . (with worker/opus/readme-runnability CLI merged), run `gpuwrf run --namelist <DIR>/namelist.input --input-dir <DIR> --output-dir runs/canary_d02_sample --domain d02 --hours 1 --compare-cpu-dir <DIR>` and confirm proofs/dimension_compare.json status=PASS with exit code 0.
